name: Aljarafe and Guadiamar Corridor Loop
name_original: Desde Sanlúcar la Mayor a Pilas – circular por Pilas, Aznalcázar y Bollullos de la Mitación
description: This moderate 32.7-mile road cycling route from Sanlucar la Mayor explores charming towns and the Guadiamar Green Corridor.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor traverse the Aljarafe region, characterized by fertile agricultural lands, extensive olive groves, and vineyards. The area offers commanding views over the Guadiamar river basin, providing a mix of open landscapes and natural features. Cyclists can explore the Guadiamar Green Corridor, a protected natural area with diverse scenery. The terrain includes gentle undulations and some longer routes with varying elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor The most popular road cycling route is Aljarafe and Guadiamar Corridor Loop, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route connects the agricultural landscapes of Aljarafe with the natural beauty of the Guadiamar Green Corridor. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Super road for racing bikes – Super road for racing bikes loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a moderate 30.9 miles (49.8 km) path. This route offers dedicated sections for road cyclists, winding through the region's characteristic agricultural fields. Local road cyclists also love the Triana Bridge – Barqueta Bridge loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a 48.0 miles (77.3 km) trail leading through varied urban and rural scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Sanlúcar La Mayor is defined by fertile agricultural lands, olive groves, and the Guadiamar Green Corridor. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sanlúcar La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;12 dedicated road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Sanlúcar La Mayor, offering diverse experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easy ride?
A: Yes, Sanlúcar La Mayor offers &lt;strong&gt;4 easy road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt;. An excellent option for a relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19341468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Las Monjas – Plaza del Ejército del Aire loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 32.9 km with gentle elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on road cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The terrain around Sanlúcar La Mayor is characterized by fertile agricultural lands, extensive olive groves, and vineyards, offering picturesque and often quiet roads. You'll encounter gentle undulations, with some longer routes featuring varying elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Triana Bridge – Barqueta Bridge loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; includes about 489 meters of elevation gain over 77.3 km.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The town's location on the "cornisa del Aljarafe" provides commanding views over the Guadiamar river basin. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3113504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas of the river, surrounding olive groves, and nearby towns.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995976657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljarafe and Guadiamar Corridor Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.6 km route that connects agricultural landscapes with natural beauty.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while road cycling in the area?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor&lt;/strong&gt; is a significant natural highlight, offering extensive routes through Mediterranean scenery, reeds, and rushes, which are home to various bird species. Within the corridor, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7389130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doblas Recreation Area - El Cardenal&lt;/a&gt;, featuring Mediterranean forest and lagoons.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1606627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River&lt;/a&gt;, and explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7086624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Loreto&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1588085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cuatrovitas&lt;/a&gt;, which reflect the region's cultural heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The best time for road cycling in Sanlúcar La Mayor is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons the only comfortable times to ride. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming landscapes.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find places to stop for refreshments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21863179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aznalcázar Pine Forest Trail – Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2858510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, offering a convenient stop. Sanlúcar la Mayor itself also boasts a reputation for delicious tapas and local cuisine.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists near the starting points of routes?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Sanlúcar la Mayor, as a town, generally offers parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations or look for public parking facilities within the town center or near the Guadiamar Green Corridor access points.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the quiet agricultural fields to the natural beauty of the Guadiamar Green Corridor, and the well-maintained roads that make for an enjoyable cycling experience.
tips: Church of impressive size from the year 1733, in the baroque style, called “popular baroque”, as opposed to “cult baroque”. Also called the "Cathedral of Aljarafe". Impressive is above all its main altarpiece, made of gilded wood.

Town located on the banks of the Guadiamar river. It has several buildings of interest such as the Church of San Pablo, the Casa Grande, the Arco de la Pescadería or the Old Fountain. Near the river bank is the Buitrago recreational area, where you can rest in the shade in the environment of the protected landscape Corredor Verde del Guadiamar.

Catholic church declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1931, built in the 13th century. Among the most outstanding elements of the interior are: the main altarpiece with the image of the Virgen del Rosario from the 17th century and the baptismal chapel presided over by the Christ of San Pedro.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route is approximately 52.6 kilometers (32.7 miles) long. With an average duration of around 2 hours and 17 minutes, it's suitable for a substantial ride, though your actual time may vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on firm, paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. While it's classified as 'moderate' with an elevation gain of about 370 meters, you can expect some undulating sections rather than consistently flat terrain. The region generally features gentle climbs and often empty roads, especially on older national routes.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass through charming towns like Pilas, Aznalcázar, and Bollullos de la Mitacion, offering glimpses of local life and architecture. Notable landmarks include the Church of Our Lady of Consolation in Sanlúcar la Mayor and the Church of Santa María. The route also offers beautiful landscapes, especially near the Guadiamar Green Corridor around Aznalcázar.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape, from the expansive, distinctly Spanish scenery with olive groves typical of Andalusia to the lush forests and botanical gardens of the Guadiamar Green Corridor. This ecologically rich area, particularly vibrant in spring, provides opportunities to observe varied aquatic birdlife, contrasting with the more agricultural and urban sections of the loop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The spring months are often ideal for cycling in this region, as the Guadiamar Green Corridor is particularly vibrant with birdlife and lush vegetation. Autumn can also offer pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if cycling during that season.

faqs.question: Where does this road cycling route start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular loop that typically starts and ends in Sanlúcar la Mayor. You should be able to find public parking within the town itself to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Guadiamar Green Corridor?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general areas of the Paisaje Protegido Corredor Verde del Guadiamar that the route passes through. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the traditional sense of a hiking trail where dogs walk alongside. If you wish to bring a dog, it would need to be safely secured in a bicycle trailer or basket, adhering to local regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths and official trails. You may find yourself on sections of the Camino de Sevilla, Calle Cruz Quiteria, Calle Escultor Illanes, Camino de Villanueva, Camino Real Viejo, and various segments of the Corredor Verde.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Pilas, Aznalcázar, and Bollullos de la Mitacion, you will find opportunities to stop for refreshments at local cafes and restaurants. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially between towns.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires a good level of fitness due to its length (52.6 km) and elevation gain (370 m). While it's on paved surfaces, it might be challenging for absolute beginners. Riders with some road cycling experience will likely find it more enjoyable.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of Consolation
